title: Requirements Engineering KPI Dashboard
content: CMMI Level 4 measurement framework with three categories: Quality KPIs (defect density; acceptance criteria coverage; traceability; ambiguity index; TBD resolution); Process KPIs (first-pass approval; volatility rate; cycle time; review effectiveness; stakeholder satisfaction); Productivity KPIs (reqs per person-day; elicitation yield; documentation efficiency; rework ratio).
tags: KPI dashboard requirements engineering CMMI quality process productivity

title: Defect Density Formula
content: Defect Density = (Review Defects + Test Defects) / Total Requirements × 100. Target: < 1 defect per 100 reqs. Warning: 1-3 per 100. Critical: > 3 per 100. Measures requirements quality. Used in SPC control charts to detect process degradation.
tags: defect density formula requirements quality KPI SPC control chart

title: Requirement Volatility Formula
content: Volatility = (Changed Requirements + Deleted Requirements) / Total Requirements × 100. Target: < 10%. Warning: 10-20%. Critical: > 20%. High volatility signals poor elicitation or unstable business context.
tags: requirement volatility formula change churn stability KPI

title: Review Effectiveness Formula
content: Review Effectiveness = Defects Found in Review / (Defects Found in Review + Defects Found in Testing) × 100. Target: > 85%. Measures how well reviews catch defects before testing. Low score = reviews are not thorough enough.
tags: review effectiveness formula defects inspection testing ratio KPI

title: Elicitation Yield Formula
content: Elicitation Yield = New Requirements Captured / Elicitation Hours spent. Target: > 10 requirements per hour. Warning: 6-10. Critical: < 6. Measures efficiency of elicitation sessions. Low score may indicate poor preparation or wrong stakeholders.
tags: elicitation yield formula productivity requirements per hour KPI

title: TBD Resolution Rate Formula
content: TBD Resolution Rate = (1 - TBDs Remaining / Total Requirements) × 100. Target: 100% before development starts. Warning: 95-99%. Critical: < 95%. Unresolved TBDs are a leading indicator of rework and scope creep.
tags: TBD resolution rate formula incomplete requirements leading indicator

title: SPC Control Chart Fundamentals
content: Components: UCL (Upper Control Limit) = Mean + 3σ; CL (Center Line) = Mean; LCL (Lower Control Limit) = Mean - 3σ. Plot metric values over time (sprints). Points outside control limits signal special-cause variation requiring investigation.
tags: SPC control chart UCL LCL mean sigma statistical process control

title: Nelson Rules for Out-of-Control Signals
content: Rule 1: Single point beyond 3σ — investigate immediately. Rule 2: 9 consecutive points on same side of center line. Rule 3: 6 consecutive points trending up or down. Rule 4: 14 consecutive alternating points. Any rule violation triggers root cause investigation.
tags: Nelson rules control chart out of control signals SPC investigation

title: Process Capability Index Cpk
content: Cpk = min[(USL - Mean) / 3σ, (Mean - LSL) / 3σ]. Interpretation: Cpk ≥ 2.0 = world-class (Six Sigma); Cpk ≥ 1.33 = capable (CMMI Level 4 target); Cpk 1.0-1.33 = marginal; Cpk < 1.0 = not capable. Calculate quarterly.
tags: Cpk process capability index formula Six Sigma CMMI USL LSL sigma

title: Sigma Level Calculation
content: Sigma Level relates to defect rate: 1σ = 69% defect-free; 3σ = 99.73%; 6σ = 99.9997% (3.4 defects per million). For requirements: calculate defects per million opportunities (DPMO) then convert to sigma level using standard table.
tags: sigma level DPMO defects per million opportunities Six Sigma quality

title: Leading vs Lagging Indicators
content: Leading indicators predict future quality (TBD count; elicitation coverage; review thoroughness). Lagging indicators measure past outcomes (defect density; volatility rate; stakeholder satisfaction). Balance both. Leading indicators enable proactive intervention before defects occur.
tags: leading lagging indicators predictive outcome metrics proactive

title: Requirements Metrics Collection Template
content: Fields per sprint: Total Requirements; New Requirements; Changed Requirements; Deleted Requirements; Review Defects; Test Defects; TBDs Remaining; Approval Cycle Time (days); Stakeholders Engaged; Elicitation Hours. Auto-calculates KPIs from raw data.
tags: metrics collection template sprint data requirements defects TBD cycle time

title: Quality KPI Targets Reference
content: Defect Density target < 1/100 reqs; Acceptance Criteria coverage 100%; Traceability Coverage > 98%; Ambiguity Index < 2%; TBD Resolution 100%. Warning thresholds are 50-80% of gap to critical. Use for setting process performance baselines.
tags: quality KPI targets acceptance criteria traceability ambiguity TBD thresholds

title: Process KPI Targets Reference
content: First-Pass Approval target > 90%; Volatility Rate target < 10%; Cycle Time target < 5 days; Review Effectiveness target > 85%; Stakeholder Satisfaction target > 4.5/5. Critical thresholds trigger mandatory root cause analysis and corrective action plan.
tags: process KPI targets first-pass approval cycle time review effectiveness satisfaction

title: Productivity KPI Targets Reference
content: Requirements per Person-Day target > 15; Elicitation Yield target > 10 req/hour; Documentation Efficiency target > 5 pages/hour; Rework Ratio target < 10%. Low productivity KPIs often correlate with high defect density — check for process capability issues.
tags: productivity KPI requirements per day elicitation yield documentation efficiency rework

title: Defect Classification Framework
content: Types: Incomplete (56% of defects — missing info); Ambiguous (26% — multiple interpretations); Inconsistent (12% — contradicts other reqs); Incorrect (wrong stakeholder intent); Untestable (unverifiable); Redundant (duplicate). Classify all defects to identify prevention priorities.
tags: defect classification types incomplete ambiguous inconsistent incorrect untestable redundant

title: PDCA Improvement Cycle for RE
content: Plan: set KPI targets and define measurement approach. Do: collect data each sprint and apply SPC. Check: analyze Cpk quarterly and review SPC charts for signals. Act: standardize improvements or implement corrective actions. Repeat quarterly.
tags: PDCA improvement cycle plan do check act requirements engineering quarterly

title: Quality Dashboard Template
content: Dashboard sections: 1) KPI scorecard (current vs target vs warning vs critical); 2) SPC control chart for defect density; 3) Trend chart for volatility rate; 4) Defect type pie chart; 5) Improvement actions tracker. Update each sprint.
tags: quality dashboard template KPI scorecard SPC trend chart improvement

title: CMMI Level 4 Metrics Checklist
content: Required: process performance baselines documented; statistical control applied to key processes; quantitative objectives defined; sub-process variation analyzed; corrective actions data-driven; Cpk ≥ 1.33 achieved and tracked quarterly.
tags: CMMI level 4 metrics checklist performance baseline quantitative Cpk statistical

title: Defect Severity Resolution Targets
content: Critical (system failure risk): resolve ≤ 24 hours. Major (significant functionality affected): resolve ≤ 48 hours. Minor (small inconsistency): resolve ≤ 1 week. Low (nice-to-have fix): add to backlog. Track all defects against these SLA targets.
tags: defect severity resolution SLA critical major minor low requirements

title: SPC Action Protocol When Out-of-Control
content: Step 1: Stop and investigate root cause immediately. Step 2: Document findings in defect log with 5-Whys or fishbone analysis. Step 3: Implement corrective action. Step 4: Verify process returns to control (within limits). Step 5: Update process baseline if change is permanent.
tags: SPC out-of-control action protocol root cause corrective action baseline update
